examples.title: Simple Auto-scaling Logic
examples.description: Demonstrates basic auto-scaling algorithm based on CPU utilization thresholds
examples.code:
import time

def auto_scale(current_instances, cpu_utilization):
    MIN_INSTANCES = 2
    MAX_INSTANCES = 20
    SCALE_UP_THRESHOLD = 70  # percent
    SCALE_DOWN_THRESHOLD = 30  # percent
    
    if cpu_utilization > SCALE_UP_THRESHOLD:
        if current_instances < MAX_INSTANCES:
            new_instances = min(current_instances + 2, MAX_INSTANCES)
            print(f"Scaling UP: {current_instances} -> {new_instances} instances")
            return new_instances
    elif cpu_utilization < SCALE_DOWN_THRESHOLD:
        if current_instances > MIN_INSTANCES:
            new_instances = max(current_instances - 1, MIN_INSTANCES)
            print(f"Scaling DOWN: {current_instances} -> {new_instances} instances")
            return new_instances
    else:
        print(f"Stable: {current_instances} instances at {cpu_utilization}% CPU")
    return current_instances

# Example simulation
instances = 5
for cpu in [25, 45, 75, 85, 90, 80, 40, 20]:
    instances = auto_scale(instances, cpu)
    time.sleep(1)
examples.explanation: This demonstrates a simple threshold-based auto-scaling mechanism that adds instances when CPU exceeds 70% and removes instances when CPU drops below 30%, with min/max bounds and hysteresis to prevent flapping.

examples.title: TCO Calculation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate Total Cost of Ownership for comparing on-premise vs cloud options
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateTCO
BEGIN
    INPUT: workload_hours, on_premise_config, cloud_config
    
    // On-Premise Costs (CapEx + OpEx over 3 years)
    hardware_cost = on_premise_config.server_cost + on_premise_config.storage_cost
    facility_cost = on_premise_config.datacenter_sqft * on_premise_config.cost_per_sqft * 3
    personnel_cost = on_premise_config.admin_staff * on_premise_config.annual_salary * 3
    maintenance_cost = hardware_cost * 0.15 * 3  // 15% annual maintenance
    power_cooling = on_premise_config.kw_usage * on_premise_config.electricity_rate * 8760 * 3
    
    on_premise_tco = hardware_cost + facility_cost + personnel_cost + maintenance_cost + power_cooling
    
    // Cloud Costs (OpEx over 3 years)
    compute_cost = cloud_config.hourly_rate * workload_hours * 3
    storage_cost = cloud_config.storage_gb * cloud_config.storage_rate_per_gb * 12 * 3
    data_transfer = cloud_config.monthly_transfer_gb * cloud_config.transfer_rate * 12 * 3
    support_cost = cloud_config.support_tier_monthly * 12 * 3
    
    cloud_tco = compute_cost + storage_cost + data_transfer + support_cost
    
    OUTPUT: on_premise_tco, cloud_tco
    IF cloud_tco < on_premise_tco THEN
        savings = on_premise_tco - cloud_tco
        PRINT "Cloud saves Rs. ", savings
    ELSE
        PRINT "On-premise is more cost-effective"
    ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm compares TCO between on-premise (CapEx model with hardware, facilities, personnel, maintenance) and cloud (OpEx model with hourly compute, storage, data transfer) over a 3-year period to determine the most cost-effective option.

examples.title: Reserved Instance Recommendation Engine
examples.description: Java program to recommend whether to use Reserved Instances based on usage patterns
examples.explanation: This program analyzes usage patterns to recommend whether Reserved Instances would be cost-effective. It calculates total costs for on-demand vs reserved pricing and recommends based on potential savings percentage.

examples.title: Cloud Cost Budget Monitor
examples.description: Algorithm for monitoring cloud spending against budget thresholds and triggering alerts
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BudgetMonitor
BEGIN
    INPUT: current_spend, budget_limit, alert_thresholds
    
    SET alert_thresholds = [0.5, 0.75, 0.90, 1.0]  // 50%, 75%, 90%, 100%
    SET alert_messages = ["Warning: 50% budget used",
                          "Alert: 75% budget used",
                          "Critical: 90% budget used",
                          "Exceeded: Budget limit reached"]
    
    percentage_used = (current_spend / budget_limit) * 100
    
    FOR i FROM 0 TO length(alert_thresholds) - 1 DO
        threshold_percentage = alert_thresholds[i] * 100
        
        IF percentage_used >= threshold_percentage THEN
            IF NOT alert_sent[threshold_percentage] THEN
                SEND_ALERT(alert_messages[i])
                alert_sent[threshold_percentage] = TRUE
                
                IF percentage_used >= 100 THEN
                    PRINT "URGENT: Stopping non-critical resources..."
                    TRIGGER_COST_CONTROLS()
                ENDIF
            ENDIF
        ENDIF
    ENDFOR
    
    daily_burn_rate = current_spend / days_elapsed
    projected_monthly = daily_burn_rate * days_in_month
    
    IF projected_monthly > budget_limit THEN
        PROJECTION_ALERT("Projected overspend: Rs." + (projected_monthly - budget_limit))
    ENDIF
    
    OUTPUT: alert_status, projected_monthly
END

PROCEDURE SEND_ALERT(message)
BEGIN
    PRINT "[ALERT] " + message
    EMAIL_NOTIFICATION(admin_email, message)
    LOG_AUDIT(message)
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm monitors cloud spending against defined budget thresholds, sending alerts at 50%, 75%, 90%, and 100% of budget utilization. It also calculates daily burn rate to project monthly spending and trigger proactive alerts.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of thresholds
